sections.paragraph:
ζῶ [contr. of the epic form ζώω via Attic ζῶ, the ζη-forms being secondary]

—1. ‘be in the state of being alive’, live Mt 27:63; Lk 24:5, 23; Ac 17:28; 20:12; Ro 7:1-3; 1 Cor 7:39; 2 Cor 4:11; Phil 1:22; 1 Th 4:15; 2 Ti 4:1; Hb 9:17; Js 4:15; Rv 1:18; 2:8; 20:5. —Freq. of God Mt 16:16; J 6:57; Ac 14:15; Ro 9:26; Rv 15:7. —By ext. to inanimate things and closely associated with ideas expressed in 3 below: ὕδωρ ζῶν living water J 4:10f; 7:38 (sayings); 1 Pt 1:3 (hope); Hb 10:20 (way).—Expressions: τὸ ζῆν life 2 Cor 1:8; juristic ζῶ ἐγώ on my life, as surely as I live Ro 14:11.

—2. ‘pass one’s life’, live Lk 2:36; 15:13; J 4:50f; Ac 1:3; 10:42; 25:19; 26:5; Ro 6:2; 14:7; 2 Cor 5:15; Gal 2:14; Col 2:20; 3:7; 2 Ti 3:12; Tit 2:12; 1 Pt 2:24.

—3. ext. of 1 and 2 ‘be alive in a manner that transcends mere physical existence’, live Mt 4:4; Lk 10:28; Ro 1:17; 6:2; 2 Cor 13:4 al.
